title: Xtra Crispy Chinese Tofu Mu Shu
description: A plant-based, fermented, aerated Chinese dish originating from Dongbei, featuring crispy tofu and mixed vegetables, served with rice and pancakes. This recipe is a post-workout friendly meal option.

ingredients.name: extra firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: In a large bowl, toss the tofu cubes with cornstarch until they are evenly coated.
Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu cubes to the skillet and cook until they are golden brown and crispy, about 5 minutes.
Remove the tofu from the skillet and set aside on a plate.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil and stir in the chopped scallions and sliced mushrooms.
Cook the scallions and mushrooms until they are softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the shredded cabbage to the skillet and cook until it is wilted, about 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and rice vinegar, and pour the mixture over the cabbage mixture in the skillet.
Stir in the cooked tofu cubes and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Warm the mu shu pancakes by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the mu shu by spreading a small amount of the tofu and vegetable mixture onto a pancake and serving immediately.
